Petition For Order Determining Interest In Property And Directing Trustee To Transfer Property.

Petition For Order To Determine Invalidity Of Alleged Amendment To Decleration Of Trust As A Result Of Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence, Fraud, Mistake, Forgery And Inadequacy Of Execution
Off Calendar Mar-19-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Barbara Jagiello for The First Unitarian Church. John Browne for Judith Lovrince. James Barringer for Robert Lee. Ken Johnson for Andre Audetat.

Mr. Johnson reported that the matter had settled, that the documents were prepared and that two new trustees were going to be appointed. The Petition For Order To Determine Invalidity Of Alledged Amendment To Declaration Of Trust, etc., and The Petition For Order Determining Interest In Property were taken off calendar. The Court signed the order approving the settlement and the order naming new successor trustees.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: F. Smith; Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

Petition For Order To Determine Invalidity Of Alleged Amendment To Decleration Of Trust As A Result Of Lack Of Capacity, Undue Influence, Fraud, Mistake, Forgery And Inadequacy Of Execution
Off Calendar Mar-05-2008 Continued To Mar-19-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ken Johnson for Andre Audetat.

Mr. Johnson stated that the global settlement had not been signed by all parties. All matters were continued to March 19, 2008. The request for approval of the settlement is set for March 19, 2008, and will not be heard on the Ex-parte calendar. Mr. Johnson will send notice regarding the date set to approve the settlement.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

MINI MINUTES: Ken Johnson appearing for beneficiary Andre Audetat and specially appearing for Mr. Browne. Barbara Jagiello appearing for The First Unitarian Universalist Society. Discovery is not completed. Matter is continued to 6/27 for status.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Answers To Deposition Questions;
Off Calendar Apr-24-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Petitioner The First Unitarian Universalist society's motion to compel answers at deposition is granted. The Court finds that the questions relating to trust administration are relevant to the subject matter of the trust contest. Petitioner's right to conduct discovery is independent of the statutory duties of the trustee. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-18-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Charles Wolff appearing for trustee and moving party. Ken Johnson appearing for beneficiary Andre Audetat. Jack Browne appearing for Lovrince claimants. Barbara Jagiello appearing for Unitarian Church. Oral argument presented by Ms. Jagiello and responded by Mr. Wolff and Mr. Johnson. Court stated the discovery issues should stay in Discovery. Counsel may want to go back to Commissioner McMath for clarification of her order. Issue on does the church has the right as a beneficiary or the right as a litigant to request for information from the trustee is submitted. Mr. Wolff will submit an order. Ms. Jagiello requested for one week to submit opposition brief. Court is giving her until 4/25. The discovery hearing is set on 4/24.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: F. Smith.

Off Calendar Dec-12-2006 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Ken Johnson and Steven Small appearing for beneficiary. Barbara Jagiello appearing for the Unitarian Church. Charles Wolff appearing for trustee H. Robert Lee. Jeffery Brown appearing for the Lovrinces. Oral argument presented. Court askes for permission to hear addition argument at the end of the calendar. All counsel agree.
At 10:50am, All counsel return to court. Additional argument presented. Matter submitted.
Mr. Brown requests for continuance for 90 days to conduct discovery on his petition. Ms. Jagiello objects to his clients' standing. Court is granting Mr. Brown's request to allow for discovery. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.
Ms. Jagiello requests for continuance on her other petition for 90 days.
